concepts.finding: Deep metric learning comprehensively covers Siamese networks, triplet networks, contrastive/triplet/N-pair/angular losses, and sampling strategies (hard mining, semi-hard mining, distance-weighted sampling).
concepts.implication: Metric learning techniques are directly applicable to learning query and document embeddings where semantically similar items should be close and dissimilar items far apart.
concepts.open_question: Which metric learning loss and sampling strategy is optimal for query-document embedding training?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Multi-similarity loss with distance-weighted sampling will outperform triplet loss with semi-hard mining by 5%+ recall@10 for query-document embedding learning.

concepts.finding: Sampling strategy (how negative examples are selected) is as important as the loss function for metric learning performance; hard negative mining is crucial but must be balanced.
concepts.implication: For query embedding training, careful selection of hard negatives (similar but irrelevant queries/documents) is critical and often more impactful than model architecture choices.
concepts.open_question: What is the optimal ratio of easy/hard/random negatives for query-document contrastive learning?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: A 1:1:1 ratio of hard:semi-hard:random negatives will outperform pure hard negative mining by 3%+ recall@10 for query-document embedding training.
